spec_match.rationale: The repaired page names and probes border-width, outline-width, column-rule-width, their corresponding style properties, computedStyleMap(), and getComputedStyle().

interactive.rationale: The authored-width slider, style selector, and none/solid toggle update the visual samples and CSSOM readout live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now demonstrates transitions through painting and non-painting style states while explaining the border used-value exception.

edge_cases.rationale: The none/hidden branch explicitly shows getComputedStyle().borderTopWidth as a used paint width that can be 0px while computedStyleMap() retains the authored border width.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus feature link for 5133099851317248 remains present.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, has no raw hex colors, and removes the previous inline onclick handler.

summary: Open critique items repaired for this concept: the page now exposes the exact width/style CSSOM contract and a meaningful none/hidden edge branch. DevTools MCP verification exercised the authored-width slider, style selector, and none/solid toggle at http://localhost:3000/v147/css-update-decoupling-of-width-and-style-properties/transition-continuity/?verify=after-final; the settled none branch showed computedStyleMap().get('border-top-width') at 9px while getComputedStyle().borderTopWidth reported 0px, then the solid branch returned both to 9px. Console and network were clean, and the feature conformance route passed 7 of 7 assertions in Chrome 148.
